# v0.1.15

Stable feature release: six-phase optimization roadmap (quality â†’ modularization â†’ performance â†’ observability â†’ CI â†’ post-roadmap audit items), with 268 passing tests and a cross-platform CI gate.

## What Changed

### Quality foundation (phase 1)

- Added `tests/test_contract_schema.py` locking the JSON contract for `search` / `research` / `doctor` success and failure shapes; schema-breaking changes now fail CI.
- Tightened `research` `gap_check` convergence: a single Context7 noise hit no longer reports `evidence_converged` / `gap_check.status: closed`; intent correction down-weights docs routing when the query only names a provider.
- Added full-flow mock E2E tests asserting `citations` only come from `evidence_items` (no discovery snippet leakage).
- Added provider fallback parameterized matrix tests covering Tavily / Firecrawl / Jina combinations.

### `service.py` modularization (phases 2â€“3)

- Split the ~2855-line `service.py` into focused modules while keeping `service.py` as a facade:
  - `research_artifacts.py`, `research_synthesis.py`, `research_fetch.py`, `research_discovery.py`
  - `research_plan.py`, `research_routing.py`, `research_keywords.py`, `research_intent.py`, `research_gap.py`
  - `research_executor.py` (stage runner), `research_fetch_batch.py` (concurrent fetch)

### Performance and cost control (phase 4)

- Concurrent candidate fetch via `asyncio.Semaphore` + `gather`, default concurrency 3 (`research_fetch_batch.py`).
- `--locale-scope cn|en|both` controls bilingual discovery so research no longer always pays for both languages.
- Candidate fetch limit parameterized by budget: quick=3 / standard=5 / deep=6.

### CLI observability (phase 5)

- `research --dry-run` previews the plan and routing without calling live providers.
- `--progress` emits `[research]` stage logs to stderr.
- Structured citations (`id`, `source_type`, `subquestion_id`, `verified`, `content_len`) and `output_schema_version: 1` in research output.

### CI and release gates (phase 6)

- GitHub Actions workflow `test.yml` with `ubuntu-latest` + `windows-latest` matrix.
- Gates: `compileall`, `npm test` (pytest + wrapper), `npm run pack:dry`.
- Windows `npm test` uses a project-local `.pytest-tmp` directory to avoid system TEMP permission issues.

### Post-roadmap audit items

- **Provider TTL cache (P2 #11)**: new `research_cache.py` with per-capability exact-match TTL cache â€” `web_fetch` 7d / `docs_search` 1h / `web_search` 10min / `main_search` uncached. Time-sensitive queries (current/recent keywords) skip `web_search`/`docs_search` caching. TTL jitter Â±10% avoids thundering herd. `SMART_SEARCH_CACHE=off` disables globally. `provider_attempts` and `stage_results` record `cache_hit`; failed fetches are not cached.
- **`zhipu-search` deprecation cycle started (P3 #15)**: every `zhipu-search` invocation now emits a `[DEPRECATED]` stderr warning with the removal schedule. `zhipu-search --help` and the CLI command table mark it deprecated. README adds a "Deprecation notices" section with the three-stage schedule (0.1.x warning / 0.2.0 remove command / 1.0.0 remove provider + config). No code removed yet.

### Test isolation

- `tests/conftest.py` now resets the process-wide provider cache between tests to prevent cross-test cache pollution.

## Upgrade

```powershell
npm install -g @blxzer/smart-search@latest
smart-search --version
```

Expected version after publish: `smart-search 0.1.15`.

## Validation

- Source checkout: `python -m compileall -q src tests`
- Source checkout: `python -m pytest tests -q` â†’ 268 passed
- npm wrapper: `npm test` â†’ 268 passed (includes pytest + wrapper + `npm run pack:dry`)
- CI matrix: ubuntu-latest + windows-latest
